title: Configurable notification icon via Platform bridge (first desktop customization slice)
consumer_purpose: Allow an operator to customize the notification icon URL without rebuilding the desktop app, proving the customization pipeline (config → store → IPC → renderer) end-to-end.
claim_atoms: Notification icon URL is no longer hardcoded in renderer source
Icon URL is configurable via electron-store with an opaque key
A new Platform method exposes the icon override to the app framework
The existing IPC channel pattern is used for the store read
Default value preserves current behavior when no override is set
No changes required to @opencode-ai/plugin, PluginLoader, or opencode core
No breaking changes to existing Platform consumers
content:
## First Desktop Customization Slice: Configurable Notification Icon

### Files to Modify (5 files, additive only)

1. **packages/desktop/src/preload/types.ts** — Add optional `getNotificationIconUrl?: () => Promise<string | null>` to `ElectronAPI` interface
2. **packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts** — Add `get-notification-icon-url` IPC handler (ipcMain.handle) that reads from electron-store. Add to `Deps` interface as optional method `getNotificationIconUrl?: () => string | null`
3. **packages/desktop/src/preload/index.ts** — Expose `getNotificationIconUrl` through contextBridge
4. **packages/desktop/src/renderer/index.tsx** — In `createPlatform()`, read from `window.api.getNotificationIconUrl()` and pass to Notification constructor. Must be optional with fallback to hardcoded URL.
5. (If Types file lives in @opencode-ai/app) — Make Platform.notify accept optional iconUrl parameter

### Design Decisions
- All changes are ADDITIVE — never remove existing code (dirty files have concurrent session edits)
- Platform method is OPTIONAL — web platform doesn't need to implement it
- URL validated as http/https before passing to Notification API
- electron-store read via existing IPC pattern (ipcMain.handle)
- Fallback to default URL on: missing store key, corrupt store, non-http URL, IPC timeout

### Evidence Plan
1. Unit: store entry can be written/read
2. IPC: channel returns expected value or null
3. Platform: createPlatform() uses configurable URL when set, falls back to default
4. Security: file:// and javascript: URLs are rejected
5. Regression: no override → default URL behavior preserved

### Attack Surface
- Persistence: corrupt store → fallback to default
- Latency: slow IPC → async fallback with default
- Security: http(s) URL validation
- Fresh install: no store entry → default URL
